surfaces.dashboard_card: bg-white border border-slate-200 rounded-lg shadow-sm hover:shadow-md transition-shadow duration-200 p-5
surfaces.glass_header: bg-white/80 backdrop-blur-xl border-b border-slate-200/50 sticky top-0 z-50
surfaces.map_overlay: bg-white/90 backdrop-blur-md border border-slate-200 rounded-lg shadow-lg p-4

inputs.form_field: border border-slate-300 rounded-md focus:ring-2 focus:ring-blue-500/20 focus:border-blue-500 transition-all
inputs.upload_zone: border-2 border-dashed border-slate-300 rounded-lg p-8 text-center hover:bg-slate-50 transition-colors flex flex-col items-center justify-center gap-4 cursor-pointer

components.status_badge: inline-flex items-center px-2.5 py-0.5 rounded-full text-xs font-medium uppercase tracking-wider

layouts.officer_dashboard: grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-3 lg:grid-cols-4 gap-4 sm:gap-6 items-start

hero_background.description: Aerial view of clean city intersection, use for public transparency map landing or main hero.

map_abstract.description: Abstract data node map, use for analytics dashboard hero or background texture.

pothole_sample.description: Sample photo for a reported issue (Citizen ticket dashboard mock data).

resolution_sample.description: Sample photo for a resolved issue (Officer fixing the street).

instructions_to_main_agent: Strictly adhere to the color palette, especially the SLA indicators (Green <50%, Yellow 50-75%, Red >75%). Avoid default Tailwind green/red/yellow without checking contrast.
Implement Leaflet/OSM map natively with clear markers. Use cluster logic for high-density complaint areas.
For the Issue Reporting form: Make the photo upload area prominent (upload_zone class) and ensure GPS coordinates visually lock in before submission.
Officer Dashboard needs the Mode B 'Control Room' grid: High density, gap-4 to gap-6, 1px borders, white solid cards against F8FAFC background.
Do NOT use 'Inter'. Add 'Outfit' (weights 400,600,700) and 'IBM Plex Sans' (weights 400,500) via Google Fonts.
Every interactive element MUST include a data-testid attribute in kebab-case (e.g. data-testid='submit-issue-button').
Ticket Status Timeline should be visual (Submitted -> Assigned -> In Progress -> Resolved -> Closed), using a vertical or horizontal stepper.
Build Shadcn-based tabs and dialogs for two-way messaging within the ticket detail view.
